title: adenia lanceolata subsp. lanceolata
intro: Adenia lanceolata subsp. lanceolata, also known as the Climbing Adenia or Wild Potato, is a species of flowering plant in the family Passifloraceae, native to tropical Africa. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The health benefits of Adenia lanceolata subsp. lanceolata include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function. The plant is often used in traditional medicine and can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or ointment, with the root being the most commonly used part for herbal preparations.
